encounter.reason: quarterly follow-up, mood decline, drinking concerns noted
encounter.location: Portland Behavioral Health Associates, 1520 NW 18th Ave, Suite 304, Portland, OR 97209

screening.name: PHQ-9 depression screening
screening.interpretation: moderate depression (worsened from 7 at prior visit in August 2022)

conditions.name: bipolar II disorder, depressive phase worsening

medications.name: lamotrigine 200 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take one 200 mg tablet by mouth daily

medications.name: quetiapine 50 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: take one 50 mg tablet by mouth at bedtime

document_references.description: Quarterly psychiatric follow-up documenting worsening depressive symptoms (PHQ-9 14, up from 7), GAD-7 of 8, stable vitals with 5 lb weight gain, therapeutic lamotrigine level, elevated triglycerides at 178 mg/dL, concern about nightly alcohol use and its potential contribution to mood destabilization, continuation of current medications, and shortened follow-up interval to 6 weeks.

diagnostic_reports.description: Metabolic monitoring panel ordered for quetiapine safety surveillance. Glucose 96 mg/dL (normal) and triglycerides 178 mg/dL (elevated, up from 148 mg/dL) were the specifically reported values. Only 2 individual components were called out in the clinical note, insufficient to back a full metabolic panel report. Triglyceride elevation noted as likely quetiapine-related with possible alcohol contribution.
